warnings: Fitted copy is accurate but too generic for CALL; it does not clearly foreground CALL’s leadership-for-learning assessment, multi-source feedback, or school-wide needs-assessment value for principals.
“Support administrators with tools and tailored services” is broad, low-proof wording and risks sounding like scaffolded marketing copy rather than specific CALL evidence.
The final headline repeats “Focus on” three times; acceptable rhetorically, but it contributes to generic/monotonous campaign copy.
The image is clean and usable, but the visible scene could be any corporate planning meeting; school leadership, principal audience, and CALL feedback assessment are only weakly signaled.
The art plate reserves a very dark top-left area while render QA reports a native CALL logo variant; final logo contrast should be checked before export.
